channel.title: Ecclesiology After à Brakel
channel.description: A personal audio feed — reflections on the church from Wilhelmus à Brakel's "The Reasonable Service of God." Read by Jason Ruis.
channel.itunes:author: Jason Ruis
channel.itunes:summary: Personal audio reflections on Reformed ecclesiology, working through à Brakel's The Reasonable Service of God. Not a public podcast — just for personal listening.

item.title: 06 — The Keys and the Ban
item.description: à Brakel on the ecclesiastical power and the keys of the kingdom of heaven — consistories, classes, synods; the first key (preaching the Word); the second key (the Christian ban and church discipline); church and magistrates (Belgic Confession Art. 36); and the exhortation to overseers to use the keys faithfully. The finale of the ecclesiology series. From "The Reasonable Service of God," Volume 2, Chapter 29.

item.title: 05 — Feed My Sheep: The Job Description
item.description: à Brakel's job description for church officers — the qualifications and six duties of pastors (praying, preaching, catechizing, visiting, sacraments, discipline), the work of ruling elders, the diaconate, and the accounting every officer will give. From "The Reasonable Service of God," Volume 2, Chapter 28.

item.title: 04 — No Throne in the Church but Christ's
item.description: à Brakel against the papacy and episcopal hierarchy — no visible head, no bishop over other shepherds, and the calling of ministers inward and outward, from the church not from magistrates. From "The Reasonable Service of God," Volume 2, Chapter 27.

item.title: 03 — All Mine Is Thine, and Thine Is Mine
item.description: à Brakel on the communion of believers with Christ and with one another — ownership, union, and communion with Christ's Person and His goods, and the communion of the militant and triumphant church. From "The Reasonable Service of God," Volume 2, Chapter 26.

item.title: 02 — You Do Not Get to Leave
item.description: à Brakel's case against forsaking the church — why you must never leave to build a purer one, and why you must not abstain from the Lord's Supper even when the church is corrupt. From "The Reasonable Service of God," Volume 2, Chapter 25, sections VIII–XXX.

item.title: 01 — There Is No Christ Without His Church
item.description: à Brakel's case for why every Christian must join the church — five proofs and five motives, from "The Reasonable Service of God," Volume 2, Chapter 25.
